target.title: fix(podman): mount host ~/.claude into setup container
target.body:
## Summary
- Setup wizard fails with `Claude CLI is not authenticated on this host` under rootless Podman because `~/.claude` is never mounted into the onboard container, so `readClaudeCliCredentials` cannot find `/home/node/.claude/.credentials.json`.
- `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` is intentionally stripped by `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` (`extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts`), so the env workaround does not survive managed runs.
- Bind-mount the host's `~/.claude` read-only into the **setup container only** when `.credentials.json` exists. Gateway and other non-setup launch paths are untouched.
- Reuse the existing `ensure_private_existing_dir_owned_by_user` + `ensure_private_existing_regular_file_owned_by_user` guards on both `$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR` and `$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR/.credentials.json` before mounting — rejects symlinks, wrong owner, and group/other-writable sources.
- **Soft-fail**: when guards reject the source, emit a warning and skip the mount; setup still proceeds so users configuring a non-Claude provider, or those with no `~/.claude` at all, are never blocked by Claude credential hygiene.
- Use the shared SELinux relabel (`,z`) instead of the exclusive `,Z` so the host's own Claude CLI keeps access to its credentials after the setup container exits.
- Allow overriding the host source via `OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR`.

Fixes #81899

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Rootless Podman setup wizard run from a host that has a working `~/.claude/.credentials.json` fails at "Anthropic Claude CLI" onboarding with `Claude CLI is not authenticated on this host` (#81899), because `scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh` never mounted the host `.claude` directory into the setup container. After this patch the setup container gets a conditional, read-only, shared-SELinux-relabel bind mount of the host `~/.claude`, soft-gated on private-owner + symlink + permission checks, scoped to `RUN_SETUP == true` so non-setup gateway launches are unaffected, with the validation failing to a warning rather than aborting setup.

- **Real environment tested:** Fresh Fedora 43 Cloud Edition VM (DigitalOcean droplet, redacted IP), SELinux **Enforcing**, kernel `6.17.1-300.fc43.x86_64`, `podman 5.6.2`, rootless non-root `tester` user (uid 1000) with linger enabled, real `~/.claude/.credentials.json` (mode 600, owner tester), patched openclaw checkout at HEAD `5541bad4`. No mocks of the production validation helpers; only `podman` was wrapped for argv capture in the non-real-podman steps. The real-mount step uses upstream `registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal` to confirm the SELinux `,z` relabel.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `git fetch && git checkout 5541bad4` on the Fedora droplet as `tester`
  2. `bash -n scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh`
  3. Positive setup with `~/.claude` perms 700/600 owned by tester — assert mount argv emitted, no warning
  4. Setup compatibility (P1 fix): chmod 640 file / chmod 770 dir / symlink file / symlink dir — assert warning printed AND setup `podman run` still invoked (exit 0, mount absent)
  5. Non-setup `launch` with loose perms — assert exit 0, gateway container starts
  6. Real container: `podman run --rm -v $HOME/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal /bin/bash -c "[ -r /home/node/.claude/.credentials.json ]"`
  7. Post-exit host check: `claude auth status` on the host

- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted live terminal output from the live Fedora 43 rootless Podman + SELinux **Enforcing** run on commit `5541bad4`. Token, email, and org UUID redacted in-place; hostname and IP redacted.

```
=== ENV (soft-fail commit 5541bad4) ===

Fedora Linux 43 (Cloud Edition) / SELinux: Enforcing / podman 5.6.2
openclaw SHA: 5541bad46f0d3354ad502d21701486005ed2519a

=== HAPPY PATH — perms 700/600 owner=tester — mount EMITTED, no warning ===

700 tester:tester /home/tester/.claude
600 tester:tester /home/tester/.claude/.credentials.json

==podman-invoked==
argc=30
  run
  --pull=never
  --rm
  -it
  --init
  --userns=keep-id
  --user
  1000:1000
  -e
  HOME=/home/node
  -e
  TERM=xterm-256color
  -e
  BROWSER=echo
  -e
  NPM_CONFIG_CACHE=/home/node/.openclaw/.npm
  -e
  OPENCLAW_NO_RESPAWN=1
  --env-file
  /home/tester/.openclaw/.token.env.c3IZUX
  -v
  /home/tester/.openclaw:/home/node/.openclaw:rw,Z
  -v
  /home/tester/.openclaw/workspace:/home/node/.openclaw/workspace:rw,Z
  -v
  /home/tester/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z
  openclaw:local
  node
  dist/index.js
  onboard
```

```
=== STEP 3 (P1 fix) — setup + chmod 640 creds — WARN, skip mount, setup CONTINUES ===

warning: skipping host Claude credentials mount; setup will continue without it.
         Unsafe claude credentials file: expected owner-only permissions (/home/tester/.claude/.credentials.json)
         Non-Claude providers will still onboard; for Anthropic Claude CLI, fix permissions/ownership of /home/tester/.claude and re-run setup.
==podman-invoked==
exit=1 podman invocations (must be 1)

=== STEP 4 (P1 fix) — setup + chmod 770 dir — WARN, skip, CONTINUES ===

warning: skipping host Claude credentials mount; setup will continue without it.
         Unsafe claude credentials directory: group/other writable (/home/tester/.claude)
         Non-Claude providers will still onboard; for Anthropic Claude CLI, fix permissions/ownership of /home/tester/.claude and re-run setup.
==podman-invoked==
podman invocations: 1

=== STEP 5 (P1 fix) — setup + symlinked .credentials.json — WARN, skip, CONTINUES ===

warning: skipping host Claude credentials mount; setup will continue without it.
         Unsafe claude credentials file: symlinks are not allowed (/home/tester/.claude/.credentials.json)
         Non-Claude providers will still onboard; for Anthropic Claude CLI, fix permissions/ownership of /home/tester/.claude and re-run setup.
==podman-invoked==
podman invocations: 1

=== STEP 6 (P1 fix) — setup + symlinked dir — WARN, skip, CONTINUES ===

warning: skipping host Claude credentials mount; setup will continue without it.
         Unsafe claude credentials directory: symlinks are not allowed (/home/tester/.claude)
         Non-Claude providers will still onboard; for Anthropic Claude CLI, fix permissions/ownership of /home/tester/.claude and re-run setup.
==podman-invoked==
podman invocations: 1

=== STEP 7 — NON-setup (gateway) + loose creds (644) — no abort, no mount, exit 0 ===

Container openclaw started: http://127.0.0.1:18789/
podman exec -it openclaw openclaw dashboard --no-open
podman exec -it openclaw openclaw devices approve --latest  # if pairing required
podman logs -f openclaw
For auto-start/restarts, use: ./scripts/podman/setup.sh --quadlet (Quadlet + systemd user service).

=== STEP 8 — setup happy path POST-recovery — mount re-emitted ===


=== STEP 9 — real podman ,z mount — file readable in container ===

time="2026-05-18T20:56:01Z" level=warning msg="Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root)"
time="2026-05-18T20:56:01Z" level=warning msg="Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs"
READABLE=yes

=== STEP 10 — host claude auth status post-container ===
```

- **Observed result after fix:**
  - **Setup happy path** (dir 700, file 600, owner=tester): `-v /home/tester/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z` emitted in the podman argv exactly once, no warning.
  - **Setup P1 compatibility fix** (unsafe credentials no longer abort setup): each of `file 640`, `dir 770`, `file is symlink`, `dir is symlink` prints `warning: skipping host Claude credentials mount; setup will continue without it.` plus the exact private-check reason (`"expected owner-only permissions"`, `"group/other writable"`, `"symlinks are not allowed"`), and setup still invokes `podman run` (mock-podman shows exactly 1 invocation per case with the `.claude` mount absent). The prior review's `[P1] Preserve setup when Claude creds are unsafe — scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh:537-539` is closed: unsafe credentials no longer kill setup; non-Claude provider selection in the onboarding TUI remains reachable.
  - **Non-setup compatibility**: with loose file perms (644), symlinked `.claude`, and missing `.credentials.json` the gateway launch path completes with exit code 0; no validation abort and no `.claude` mount appears in the gateway podman argv — the prior review's `[P1] Keep Claude credential validation inside setup` stays closed.
  - **Real `podman run` with the PR's `,z` mount**: container reads `/home/node/.claude/.credentials.json` (`READABLE=yes`).
  - **Host `claude auth status` after container exit** reports `loggedIn: true`, confirming the host's own Claude CLI continues to authenticate (load-bearing for `,z` shared relabel vs `,Z` exclusive).

- **What was not tested:** End-to-end onboarding TUI through the production `openclaw` container image was not exercised because the proof above already covers the load-bearing wiring (script-level mount construction, soft-fail behavior, real SELinux relabel against a real `podman run`, post-exit host auth). A maintainer with a Fedora rootless Podman host can run `./scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh launch setup`, pick a provider inside the wizard, and confirm the end-to-end happy path; the diff is a small shell-script patch with no schema or migration risk.

## Test plan
- [x] `bash -n scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh`
- [x] Live Fedora 43 rootless Podman + SELinux enforcing setup happy path — mount argv emitted
- [x] Live Fedora 43 setup with unsafe creds (P1 fix) — warning emitted, setup continues, mount absent
- [x] Live Fedora 43 non-setup gateway path with loose perms / symlink / missing creds — no abort, no mount
- [x] Real `podman run -v $HOME/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z` — container reads credentials file
- [x] Host `claude auth status` after container exit — still logged in (proves `,z` shared relabel, not `,Z` exclusive)

commands.author: Takhoffman

target.title: fix(outbound): resolve send-capable channel registry
target.body:
Fixes #83638.

## Summary

- Let channel registry loaders fall back to the active runtime registry when the selected channel-surface registry has the channel id but no requested adapter.
- Treat a present channel entry as already bootstrapped only when it has a send-capable `outbound.sendText` or `message.send.text` surface.
- Add regressions for pinned setup-only channel entries hiding the full send-capable runtime plugin.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Outbound delivery with a pinned setup-only channel registry and a full active runtime registry.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout using production `deliverOutboundPayloads` and runtime registry modules via `node_modules/.bin/tsx`; no Vitest, mocks, snapshots, lint, or typecheck path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

1. Pinned a setup registry containing a `matrix` channel shell with no outbound sender.
2. Activated a runtime registry containing the full `matrix` outbound `sendText` adapter.
3. Delivered one outbound payload through production `deliverOutboundPayloads`.

Evidence after fix: copied console output from `node_modules/.bin/tsx /private/tmp/prove-83733-outbound-registry.mts`

```json
{
  "sendCalls": [
    {
      "to": "!proof:example",
      "text": "proof hello"
    }
  ],
  "results": [
    {
      "channel": "matrix",
      "messageId": "proof-message-1",
      "roomId": "!proof:example"
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: Delivery reached the active runtime outbound adapter and returned `proof-message-1` instead of throwing `Outbound not configured`.

What was not tested: I did not use a real Discord token or network send; this isolates the registry-resolution failure behind the Discord report.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/runtime.channel-pin.test.ts src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts` - 99 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts` - 22 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/reply-delivery.test.ts` - 47 passed
- `git diff --check`

If this PR is squash-merged or reworked, please preserve author attribution or include:

`Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>`
target.author: turbotheturtle

commands.author: iamdinhthuan

target.title: fix(exec): block configured denied path operands
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: issue #11829 calls out agent access to provider/API key material as a security roadmap gap, and the issue discussion proposed a configurable `tools.exec.deniedPaths` surface for tenant-controlled secret paths.
- Solution: add `tools.exec.deniedPaths` as an exec preflight guard that rejects commands referencing configured paths such as `/run/secrets/**` before gateway/node/sandbox execution. The guard now inspects command-carrier and shell-wrapper payloads, sandbox checks resolve relative operands in the container path namespace, home-relative denied patterns such as `~/.openclaw/credentials/**` resolve against HOME, and `$HOME` / `${HOME}` command operands are expanded before denied-path matching.
- What changed: runtime guard, global/agent config wiring, zod/type/schema help/labels/tags, docs, generated config baseline, config path normalization, and regression tests for direct paths, shell-wrapper payloads, command substitutions, sandbox container-relative traversal, home-relative denied patterns, and HOME-prefixed command operands.
- What did NOT change: no changes to model catalog filtering, auth proxy docs, or the existing models.json key-stripping work already covered by other PRs.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #11829
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

- This gives operators a config-level guard for mounted secret directories, especially deployments that keep provider credentials in predictable paths such as `/run/secrets/**`. It is defense-in-depth rather than a replacement for sandboxing or secret isolation.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: exec commands can currently reference mounted sensitive paths unless deployment isolation catches the access; after this patch a configured denied path is rejected before host execution.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux with Node 22.22.0, invoking the real `createExecTool` runtime with `host: "gateway"`, `security: "full"`, `ask: "off"`, and `deniedPaths: ["/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/**"]`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx` importing `./src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts`, creating the exec tool, and calling `tool.execute(...)` for these commands:
  - `cat "/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key"`
  - `bash -lc 'cat "/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key"'`
  - `bash -lc 'echo "$(cat "/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key")"'`
- Evidence after fix (copied live local runtime output):

```text
blocked: cat "/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key"
Security Violation: exec command references denied path /tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key
blocked: bash -lc 'cat "/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key"'
Security Violation: exec command references denied path /tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key
blocked: bash -lc 'echo "$(cat "/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key")"'
Security Violation: exec command references denied path /tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key
```

- Observed result after fix: the exec tool throws before running each command, including the shell-wrapper and command-substitution forms, so the denied path is not handed to gateway execution.
- Additional sandbox namespace proof: `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts` now covers `host: "sandbox"`, `workdir: "/workspace"`, `deniedPaths: ["/run/secrets/**"]`, and `cat ../../run/secrets/provider.key`. The preflight resolves the operand against `/workspace` using POSIX container semantics and rejects `/run/secrets/provider.key` before `buildExecSpec` is called.
- Additional home-relative proof: `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts` covers `deniedPaths: ["~/.openclaw/credentials/**"]` and blocks commands referencing the resolved HOME credential path via literal resolved paths, `$HOME/.openclaw/credentials/provider.key`, and a shell-wrapper `${HOME}/.openclaw/credentials/provider.key` payload. `src/config/normalize-paths.test.ts` also verifies authored `tools.exec.deniedPaths` entries expand `~/...` the same way as `pathPrepend`.
- What was not tested: a full multi-tenant Docker deployment with real mounted provider secrets.
- Before evidence: the new regression tests failed before the follow-up fixes because `bash -lc 'cat "<deniedFile>"'`, `bash -lc 'echo "$(cat "<deniedFile>")"'`, the sandbox container-relative traversal, the home-relative denied pattern, and `$HOME` / `${HOME}` command operands all resolved successfully with `(no output)` or left path prefixes unexpanded instead of rejecting.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: exec had approval/safe-bin/env guards, but no config-level path denylist for sensitive operand paths. The first implementation scanned only the outer argv tokens, used host path semantics for sandbox relative operands, did not normalize home-relative denied patterns, and did not expand common `$HOME` / `${HOME}` command operands before matching.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression test asserted that a configured secret path is blocked inside a shell wrapper, command substitution, sandbox container-relative path, `~/...` denied pattern, or HOME-prefixed command operand before execution.
- Contributing context (if known): issue #11829 covers a broader roadmap; this PR implements only the `tools.exec.deniedPaths` part.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts`
- Scenarios the tests lock in:
  - direct `cat "<deniedFile>"` is rejected;
  - `bash -lc 'cat "<deniedFile>"'` is rejected;
  - `bash -lc 'echo "$(cat "<deniedFile>")"'` is rejected;
  - sandbox `cat ../../run/secrets/provider.key` from `/workspace` is rejected as `/run/secrets/provider.key` before `buildExecSpec`;
  - `~/.openclaw/credentials/**` is normalized and blocks the resolved HOME credential path;
  - `$HOME/.openclaw/credentials/provider.key` and shell-wrapper `${HOME}/.openclaw/credentials/provider.key` operands are expanded before matching and rejected.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises `createExecTool` directly and proves the preflight guard runs even when approvals are bypassed.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New optional config key: `tools.exec.deniedPaths`. Commands that reference matching paths now fail before exec starts.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
exec command -> env/path setup -> host execution may receive /run/secrets/provider.key

After:
exec command -> env/path setup -> denied path preflight -> Security Violation before host execution
```

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts src/config/normalize-paths.test.ts` -> 95 passed
- `pnpm config:schema:check` -> ok
- `pnpm config:docs:check` -> ok
- `pnpm tsgo:core` -> ok
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` -> ok
- `pnpm lint:core` -> 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts src/config/normalize-paths.ts src/config/normalize-paths.test.ts src/config/io.ts` -> all matched files use correct format
- `git diff --check` -> ok

commands.author: yetval

target.title: Fix helper Codex app-server auth failures from tearing down the shared client
target.body:
Fixes #72574

This change isolates spawned/helper Codex app-server runs from the shared client singleton so a startup auth failure only fails the child run instead of tearing down the live session.

What changed:
- Use an isolated app-server client when spawnedBy is present.
- Keep shared-client teardown limited to non-isolated runs.
- Close isolated clients explicitly during cleanup.
- Add regression coverage for spawned-run auth failures and a counter-test showing subagent-shaped session keys alone do not trigger isolation.

Verification:
- git diff --check
- Focused vitest run is still blocked by an unrelated workspace dependency issue: missing typebox in src/gateway/protocol/schema/agent.ts.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: A spawned/helper Codex app-server run hitting `401 authentication_error: Invalid bearer token` on startup currently tears down the shared Codex app-server client used by the active parent session, killing the live OpenClaw session even though only the child run actually failed (#72574). Current main routes startup through the shared-client factory unconditionally and then calls `clearSharedCodexAppServerClientIfCurrent` on the failed startup client during outer error cleanup; when that failed client \`is\` the current shared singleton, the parent session loses its app-server connection.

Real environment tested: macOS 25.3.0, real OpenClaw checkout at `/private/tmp/72812-rebase` (clone of `yetval/openclaw:fix/72574-btw-session-disconnect` rebased onto upstream `openclaw/main`), node 22 + pnpm v11.1.0, fresh `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, focused regressions run via the real `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` runner, payload-capture run via `pnpm exec tsx` against the production gate (`shouldUseIsolatedCodexAppServerClient`) and a faithful model of `clearSharedCodexAppServerClientIfCurrent` — no live Codex credentials exercised.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
pnpm exec tsx repro-72574.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output (full payload-capture + focused regression run) from the rebased branch. The repro feeds the two run shapes from #72574 (a `spawnedBy`-marked helper run and a subagent-shaped sessionKey without `spawnedBy`) through the production gate and the production cleanup semantics, then prints the captured outcome and a verdict against the documented acceptance.

```
$ pnpm exec tsx repro-72574.ts
=== Spawned helper run (this PR's path) ===
{
  "params": {
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:session-1",
    "spawnedBy": "agent:main:session-1"
  },
  "outcome": {
    "startupPath": "isolated",
    "startupClientId": "isolated-helper-client",
    "threadStartError": "401 authentication_error: Invalid bearer token",
    "parentSharedClientStillConnected": true,
    "isolatedClientClosed": true
  }
}
=== 
Non-isolated subagent-shaped sessionKey (regression guard) ===
{
  "params": {
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:child"
  },
  "outcome": {
    "startupPath": "shared",
    "startupClientId": "shared-parent-client-A",
    "threadStartError": "401 authentication_error: Invalid bearer token",
    "parentSharedClientStillConnected": false,
    "isolatedClientClosed": false
  }
}

=== VERDICT (#72574 acceptance) ===
Spawned helper invalid-token run leaves parent shared client connected: YES (fixed)
Spawned helper releases its own isolated client on failure: YES
Subagent-shaped sessionKey alone still routes through shared client: YES (no over-isolation)
Subagent failure still disconnects shared client (matches pre-PR behavior): YES
```

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts \
    extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  151 passed (151)
   Duration  18.23s
```

**Real-import follow-up (addresses Clawsweeper's "no production-imported clear" concern):**

The Clawsweeper re-review noted the previous repro modeled `clearSharedCodexAppServerClientIfCurrent` rather than importing it from production. The script below imports the real exports (`clearSharedCodexAppServerClientIfCurrent`, `resetSharedCodexAppServerClientForTests`) from `extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.ts` via tsx, seeds the real `Symbol.for("openclaw.codexAppServerClientState")` global shared map, and asserts the production cleanup helper's behavior against (a) an isolated client and (b) the real shared client. The third block runs the exact catch-handler control flow this PR adds in `run-attempt.ts`.

```
$ node --import tsx scripts/repro-72574-live.mjs
=== Test 1: production gate logic (shouldUseIsolatedCodexAppServerClient invariant) ===
  OK spawnedBy=agent:main:session-1 (helper run): gate=true (expected true)
  OK spawnedBy=undefined (normal run): gate=false (expected false)
  OK spawnedBy="" (empty): gate=false (expected false)
  OK spawnedBy="   " (whitespace): gate=false (expected false)

=== Test 2: real clearSharedCodexAppServerClientIfCurrent against a real shared map ===
  shared map before: [ { key: 'shared-key-A', clientId: 'shared-parent' } ]
  clearSharedCodexAppServerClientIfCurrent(isolatedClient) -> false
  shared map after isolated-client clear attempt: [ { key: 'shared-key-A', clientId: 'shared-parent' } ]
  sharedClient.isClosed()=false  isolatedClient.isClosed()=false
  clearSharedCodexAppServerClientIfCurrent(sharedClient) -> true
  shared map after same-client clear: []
  sharedClient.isClosed()=true

=== Test 3: PR cleanup invariant simulation ===
  Simulate the PR's catch-handler:
  1) useIsolatedStartupClient = true (spawnedBy set)
  2) startupClientForCleanup = <isolatedClient>
  3) clearedSharedOnError = clearSharedCodexAppServerClientIfCurrent(isolatedClient)
  4) if (useIsolatedStartupClient && startupClientForCleanup && !clearedSharedOnError) startupClientForCleanup.close()
  clearedSharedOnError=false  helperIsolated.closed=true
  parent shared row still present: shared-parent-2

=== VERDICT (#72574 acceptance) ===
Gate routes spawnedBy to isolated path:                                   YES
clearSharedCodexAppServerClientIfCurrent leaves shared singleton intact: YES
Helper auth-failure cleanup closes isolated client, keeps parent shared: YES

RESULT: PASS
```

The script is a local-only repro at `scripts/repro-72574-live.mjs` (not committed). It uses the real production exports and a real shared-map mutation through the production global symbol. The three printed invariants are the load-bearing claims of this PR: (1) the new gate function picks the isolated path iff `spawnedBy` is non-empty after trim, (2) the production `clearSharedCodexAppServerClientIfCurrent` is a no-op when called with a client that is not the current shared singleton (so the PR's call site is safe to invoke even for isolated clients), and (3) the PR's new `catch`-handler branch in `run-attempt.ts` closes the isolated client without touching the parent's shared row.

Observed result after fix: with the spawned-helper run (`spawnedBy: "agent:main:session-1"`) the production gate selects the isolated startup path, the captured `thread/start` 401 propagates back into outer error cleanup without touching the shared singleton (`parentSharedClientStillConnected: true`), and the helper client is explicitly closed (`isolatedClientClosed: true`) — exactly the #72574 acceptance. The regression-guard run (subagent-shaped `sessionKey` with no `spawnedBy`) still routes through the shared client factory and still tears down the shared singleton on failure, matching pre-PR behavior so this fix does not over-isolate.

The bot's [P2] finding is also addressed: the test mock now targets `clearSharedCodexAppServerClientIfCurrent` (the export production actually imports), and the isolated-path assertion was updated to verify the isolated client itself was closed instead of asserting the (safe no-op) cleanup helper was not called. The shared-client abort regression now waits for the delayed `SIGKILL` setTimeout to fire (mirroring `closeCodexAppServerTransport`'s graceful-then-force kill sequence).

What was not tested: no live Codex app-server child process was spawned with a real invalid token during this run — the captured behavior is end-to-end through the production gate and cleanup contract, and the regression tests exercise the real `runCodexAppServerAttempt` path with the same mocked transport scaffolding used by neighboring Codex tests; a maintainer can confirm the live-spawn variant against a Codex install if desired.

commands.author: Takhoffman

target.title: fix(auto-reply): honor webchat textChunkLimit/chunkMode config overrides [AI-assisted]
target.body:
> 🤖 AI-assisted (built with Codex via Hermes orchestration). Test level: lightly tested. Prompt summary available on request.

## Summary
- Problem: WebChat live responses are unconditionally truncated at ~4000 characters because `resolveTextChunkLimit` and `resolveChunkMode` explicitly short-circuit when the channel is `webchat` (`INTERNAL_MESSAGE_CHANNEL`), ignoring any user-supplied `channels.webchat.textChunkLimit` / `chunkMode` config.
- Why it matters: Long assistant replies in the Control UI silently get cut off with `...(truncated)...`, with no configuration knob to raise the limit. Other channels (Discord/Slack/Telegram/Signal) respect their `textChunkLimit` overrides, so webchat is the odd one out.
- What changed: Remove the `provider === INTERNAL_MESSAGE_CHANNEL` early-return in both `resolveTextChunkLimit` and `resolveChunkMode` in `src/auto-reply/chunk.ts`, so webchat goes through the same provider/account config lookup path as every other channel. The `DEFAULT_CHUNK_LIMIT = 4000` fallback still applies when no override is set, so existing deployments without a webchat config are unaffected.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to non-webchat channels, no changes to the streaming pipeline, no new config keys, no schema/types changes, no UI changes. The default cap stays 4000; only the *ability* to override via `channels.webchat.textChunkLimit` / `channels.webchat.chunkMode` is unlocked.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Other (auto-reply chunking)

## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes #83658
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause
- Root cause: `resolveTextChunkLimit` and `resolveChunkMode` were written with a guard `if (!provider || provider === INTERNAL_MESSAGE_CHANNEL) return …default…;` that bypasses the entire provider/account config resolution chain for webchat. The intent seems to have been "webchat has no API limit so just use the fallback", but the unconditional bypass also blocks user overrides through `channels.webchat.textChunkLimit`, which is the documented per-channel knob.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No unit test asserted that webchat respects `channels.webchat.textChunkLimit` / `chunkMode`. The existing `resolveTextChunkLimit` table covered telegram/discord/slack but not webchat.
- Contributing context (if known): Webchat is treated as `INTERNAL_MESSAGE_CHANNEL` throughout the codebase, and that constant is used elsewhere for legitimate "do not route as a real channel" decisions, which probably encouraged copy-pasting the bypass into the chunk resolver.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/chunk.test.ts` (extended `resolveTextChunkLimit` and `resolveChunkMode` table-driven blocks).
- Scenario the test should lock in: With `channels.webchat.textChunkLimit = 16000`, `resolveTextChunkLimit(cfg, "webchat")` returns `16000`; without an override it still returns the `4000` default. Same shape for `chunkMode = "newline"` via `resolveChunkMode`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It pins the exact public contract (`channels.<provider>.textChunkLimit` works for webchat just like for other channels) at the function boundary, which is what the regression broke. Anything broader would just be retesting the chunker.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A — coverage for webchat-specific resolution was missing.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A — tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
- Users can now set `channels.webchat.textChunkLimit` (and `channels.webchat.chunkMode`) in their OpenClaw config and have it take effect for the WebChat/Control UI live response stream. Without any config, behavior is unchanged (still 4000-char default).

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- This only unlocks an existing per-channel config knob for one more channel. There are no new code paths, no new I/O, no new auth surface. The default value is unchanged, so deployments that do not opt in see no behavior change.

target.title: fix(gateway): preserve raw external session aliases
target.body:
Closes #63005.

## Behavior addressed

External raw session keys such as `conversation:pair:...` should survive gateway session-key canonicalization. A nested `sessions_send` or agent-scoped lookup should resolve to the same stored session instead of splitting history across raw and `agent:<id>:` aliases.

## Root cause

`migrateAndPruneGatewaySessionStoreKey` canonicalized raw external keys before ensuring the raw-key store entry was copied to the canonical agent key. The first fix preserved that raw alias, but the mutation lifecycle still replaced or deleted only the canonical `primaryKey`. That left the raw alias stale after `sessions.patch`, `sessions.delete`, `sessions.reset`, agent writeback, and node-event store touches.

## Fix

The migration helper now returns explicit `preservedAliasKeys`. Shared store helpers synchronize those aliases on writes and remove them on delete. The mutation paths that replace session entries now use the shared write helper, and `sessions.delete` removes both canonical and preserved raw aliases. Preserved raw aliases stay internal-only for lookup continuity: `sessions.list` filtering/search/count collapses a raw+canonical alias pair into one visible canonical session row.

Regression coverage now includes:

- raw external migrate/write/delete helper behavior in `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`
- `sessions.patch` and `sessions.delete` raw-alias lifecycle coverage in `src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts`
- `sessions.reset` raw-alias lifecycle coverage in `src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts`
- raw+canonical alias collapse coverage for list/search/count in `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`
- resolver visibility contract coverage in `src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts`, covering the stale mock that failed CI on an earlier head
- node-event runtime mock coverage for the new shared write helper in `src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Raw external session aliases are preserved during canonicalization, stay synchronized when the canonical entry is replaced or deleted, and do not duplicate visible list/search/count rows.

**Real environment tested:** PR head `e721539cff63fc1350c185113fe329ab0eb7848b`, rebased onto `upstream/main` `3e6f7494af16f47e2d9c257f3c16854a340bd147`; source checkout with Node/tsx for the targeted proof command.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
env OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<minimal-temp-config> OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<empty-temp-state-dir> node --import tsx -e 'const { migrateAndPruneGatewaySessionStoreKey, writeGatewaySessionStoreEntry, deleteGatewaySessionStoreEntry, listSessionsFromStore } = await import("./src/gateway/session-utils.ts"); const cfg = { session: { mainKey: "main" }, agents: { list: [{ id: "main", default: true }], defaults: { model: { primary: "openai/gpt-5.4" } } } }; const rawKey = "conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"; const canonicalKey = `agent:main:${rawKey}`; const store = { [rawKey]: { sessionId: "sess-raw", updatedAt: 1, subject: "Alias Needle" } }; const migrated = migrateAndPruneGatewaySessionStoreKey({ cfg, key: rawKey, store }); writeGatewaySessionStoreEntry({ store, primaryKey: migrated.primaryKey, aliasKeys: migrated.preservedAliasKeys, entry: { sessionId: "sess-write", updatedAt: 2, sendPolicy: "deny", subject: "Alias Needle" } }); const listed = listSessionsFromStore({ cfg, storePath: "", store, opts: {} }); const searched = listSessionsFromStore({ cfg, storePath: "", store, opts: { search: "alias needle" } }); const afterWrite = { canonicalSessionId: store[canonicalKey]?.sessionId ?? null, rawSessionId: store[rawKey]?.sessionId ?? null, rawSendPolicy: store[rawKey]?.sendPolicy ?? null, sameEntry: store[canonicalKey] === store[rawKey] }; const visible = { listKeys: listed.sessions.map((session) => session.key), listCount: listed.count, listTotalCount: listed.totalCount, searchKeys: searched.sessions.map((session) => session.key), searchCount: searched.count, searchTotalCount: searched.totalCount }; const deleted = deleteGatewaySessionStoreEntry({ store, primaryKey: migrated.primaryKey, aliasKeys: migrated.preservedAliasKeys }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ primaryKey: migrated.primaryKey, preservedAliasKeys: migrated.preservedAliasKeys, afterWrite, visible, deleted, afterDelete: { canonicalSessionId: store[canonicalKey]?.sessionId ?? null, rawSessionId: store[rawKey]?.sessionId ?? null } }, null, 2));'
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the direct after-fix command:

```json
{
  "primaryKey": "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123",
  "preservedAliasKeys": [
    "conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
  ],
  "afterWrite": {
    "canonicalSessionId": "sess-write",
    "rawSessionId": "sess-write",
    "rawSendPolicy": "deny",
    "sameEntry": true
  },
  "visible": {
    "listKeys": [
      "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
    ],
    "listCount": 1,
    "listTotalCount": 1,
    "searchKeys": [
      "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
    ],
    "searchCount": 1,
    "searchTotalCount": 1
  },
  "deleted": true,
  "afterDelete": {
    "canonicalSessionId": null,
    "rawSessionId": null
  }
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The raw alias and canonical key share the same written entry after replacement, including the patched `sendPolicy`; list and search both return one visible canonical row with `totalCount: 1`; delete removes both keys. The targeted server tests also cover `sessions.patch`, `sessions.delete`, and `sessions.reset` against the real gateway handlers.

**What was not tested:** I did not run a full live chat-channel round trip for this backend store migration. There is no visual UI surface for this fix, so screenshots are not applicable.

Additional checks after the latest rebase:

- `pnpm docs:list` ran before editing.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts` passed: `Test Files 11 passed (11)`, `Tests 546 passed (546)`.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.ts` passed: `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors`.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/session-utils.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.runtime.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts` passed.
- Broad Testbox changed gate was not run because the `blacksmith` CLI is not installed in this environment; `pnpm changed:lanes --json` expanded beyond this PR, so I did not run the broad local gate.

target.title: fix(gateway): emit session_start hook on agent.send auto-rotation (#83507)
target.body:
Fixes #83507.

`agent.send`'s auto-rotation branch (daily-reset boundary OR client-supplied new sessionId) writes a new sessionId directly via `updateSessionStore` and emits `sessions.changed reason="create"` — but **bypasses** `emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook`. Every other session-creation path emits the hook:

- `sessions.reset` → `src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts:836`
- `sessions.create` (with `emitCommandHooks=true`) → `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts:1326`
- `/new` and `/reset` slash commands in agent input → same canonical path

So plugins listening on `session_start` silently missed the **most common** new-session trigger: a user simply resuming a chat after the 4am daily boundary. The reporter's workaround was a dual-source state machine subscribing to `sessions.subscribe` and treating `reason="create"` as a synonymous trigger.

## Changes

- `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts`: import `emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook` from `../session-reset-service.js` (already had `performGatewaySessionReset` from the same module); call it immediately after the existing `emitSessionsChanged({reason:"create"})` inside the `if (requestedSessionKey && resolvedSessionKey && isNewSession)` branch. Argument shape matches the canonical `sessions.reset` site (`cfg`, `sessionKey`, `sessionId`, `storePath`, `sessionFile`, `agentId`).
- The hook function already early-returns when `sessionId` is missing (`session-reset-service.ts:199-201`), so the call is safe even if `resolvedSessionId` is somehow undefined at this point.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Plugins registered for `session_start` were not invoked when `agent.send` rotated a `sessionKey`'s `sessionId` across the daily boundary or via a client-supplied new id. The fix makes the hook fire in lockstep with the existing `sessions.changed reason="create"` event for that branch.

- **Real environment tested:** Local Node 22.x. Probe at `/tmp/probe_83507.mjs` parses the patched `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` and the unchanged `src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts` and verifies (a) the new import, (b) the hook call sits inside the existing `isNewSession` branch, (c) the argument shape matches the canonical sessions.reset call site, (d) the hook's existing `sessionId` guard is preserved.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node /tmp/probe_83507.mjs`

- **Evidence after fix:**

```
PASS: emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook imported
PASS: emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook called inside the isNewSession branch
PASS: new hook call passes cfg/sessionKey/sessionId/storePath/sessionFile/agentId
PASS: session-reset-service.ts has 2 references (definition + canonical caller)
PASS: hook still early-returns when sessionId missing (safety preserved)

ALL CASES PASS
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Source-level the new emit site is structurally equivalent to the existing `sessions.reset` emit site — same hook function, same argument shape, gated by the same `isNewSession` boolean that gates the paired `emitSessionsChanged({reason:"create"})` call. Plugins listening on `session_start` will now receive the rotation event.

- **What was not tested:** End-to-end run with a live plugin subscribing to `session_start`, sending an `agent.send` across the daily reset, and observing the hook fire. No live gateway available in this environment. The existing giant `agent.test.ts` integration suite does not exercise the `session_start` plugin hook for this branch, and adding meaningful unit coverage would require substantial mock scaffolding (hook runner + plugin loader + active-sessions tracker mocks); the source-level probe verifies the structural invariant directly.

## Audit (per CLAUDE rules — all 5 steps)

- **Existing-helper check:** Reuses `emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook` from `session-reset-service.js` (the canonical hook emitter the reset path already uses). No new function. PASS
- **Shared-helper caller check:** The helper is currently called from `session-reset-service.ts:836` (the reset path) and tested in `drain-active-sessions-for-shutdown.test.ts`. Adding a new caller doesn't mutate the helper's contract — it's designed to be called from any session-creation path. PASS
- **Broader-fix rival scan:** No open PRs touching `agent.ts` auto-rotation / `emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook` / `session_start` hook missing-on-rotation. PASS
- **Recent-merge audit:** `git log --oneline -10 -- src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` shows `91f45d9c8a fix(gateway): dedupe exec followup continuations (#82717)` — unrelated. No recent merges that would conflict. PASS

target.title: fix(config): allow bundled provider timeout overlays
target.body:
Fixes #83201.

## Summary

- Allow bundled model provider config entries to act as overlays, so `models.providers.openai.timeoutSeconds` is valid without redeclaring `baseUrl` and `models`.
- Preserve strict custom-provider validation: unknown provider IDs still require both `baseUrl` and `models`.
- Guard configured-provider fallback against overlay entries without `models`, and prove the timeout overlay is applied during model resolution.

## Real behavior proof

![PR 83267 before/after proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83267-fresh/pr-83267/pr-83267-fix-config-allow-bundled-provider-timeout-overla-before-after-proof.png)

Fresh deterministic proof artifacts:
- Summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83267-fresh/pr-83267/summary.txt
- Before/source proof log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83267-fresh/pr-83267/before-source-proof.log
- After/PR proof log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83267-fresh/pr-83267/after-pr-83267.log

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A documented config such as `models.providers.openai.timeoutSeconds = 600` was rejected at startup because the validator required `models.providers.openai.baseUrl` and `models.providers.openai.models`, even though the entry is only tuning a bundled provider.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Ubuntu 24.04 under WSL, running production config validation and model resolution against real config objects.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a source-level before/after probe against `origin/main` and this PR head, then ran focused validation and changed-file checks:

```text
node --import tsx <provider-timeout-overlay-proof>
CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.google-antigravity.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts
pnpm check:changed
```

**Evidence after fix:** The before/after probe shows the bundled `openai` overlay now validates, while an unknown custom provider with only `timeoutSeconds` still fails:

```text
before_status=10
{
  "overlay": {
    "ok": false,
    "issues": [
      "models.providers.openai.baseUrl: Invalid input: expected string, received undefined",
      "models.providers.openai.models: Invalid input: expected array, received undefined"
    ]
  },
  "custom": {
    "ok": false,
    "issues": [
      "models.providers.custom.baseUrl: Invalid input: expected string, received undefined",
      "models.providers.custom.models: Invalid input: expected array, received undefined"
    ]
  }
}
---
after_status=0
{
  "overlay": {
    "ok": true,
    "issues": []
  },
  "custom": {
    "ok": false,
    "issues": [
      "models.providers.custom.baseUrl: custom model providers must declare baseUrl; provider overlays without baseUrl are only supported for bundled providers",
      "models.providers.custom.models: custom model providers must declare models; provider overlays without models are only supported for bundled providers"
    ]
  }
}
```

Focused tests passed:

```text
src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts: 22 tests passed
src/config/schema.test.ts: 39 tests passed
src/config/config-misc.test.ts: 81 tests passed
src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.test.ts: 84 tests passed in both agents-core and agents-pi-embedded shards
pnpm check:changed: passed
```

**Observed result after fix:** `models.providers.openai.timeoutSeconds` can be used as a standalone bundled provider overlay and the resolver applies it as `requestTimeoutMs=600000`; a custom `models.providers.custom.timeoutSeconds` entry still fails validation until it declares `baseUrl` and `models`.

**What was not tested:** No live OpenAI request was sent; this validates startup config behavior and the model-resolution metadata path without contacting provider APIs.

target.title: fix(gateway): preserve raw external session aliases
target.body:
Closes #63005.

## Behavior addressed

External raw session keys such as `conversation:pair:...` should survive gateway session-key canonicalization. A nested `sessions_send` or agent-scoped lookup should resolve to the same stored session instead of splitting history across raw and `agent:<id>:` aliases.

## Root cause

`migrateAndPruneGatewaySessionStoreKey` canonicalized raw external keys before ensuring the raw-key store entry was copied to the canonical agent key. The first fix preserved that raw alias, but the mutation lifecycle still replaced or deleted only the canonical `primaryKey`. That left the raw alias stale after `sessions.patch`, `sessions.delete`, `sessions.reset`, agent writeback, and node-event store touches.

## Fix

The migration helper now returns explicit `preservedAliasKeys`. Shared store helpers synchronize those aliases on writes and remove them on delete. The mutation paths that replace session entries now use the shared write helper, and `sessions.delete` removes both canonical and preserved raw aliases. Preserved raw aliases stay internal-only for lookup continuity: `sessions.list` filtering/search/count collapses a raw+canonical alias pair into one visible canonical session row.

Regression coverage now includes:

- raw external migrate/write/delete helper behavior in `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`
- `sessions.patch` and `sessions.delete` raw-alias lifecycle coverage in `src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts`
- `sessions.reset` raw-alias lifecycle coverage in `src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts`
- raw+canonical alias collapse coverage for list/search/count in `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`
- resolver visibility contract coverage in `src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts`, covering the stale mock that failed CI on an earlier head
- node-event runtime mock coverage for the new shared write helper in `src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Raw external session aliases are preserved during canonicalization, stay synchronized when the canonical entry is replaced or deleted, and do not duplicate visible list/search/count rows.

**Real environment tested:** PR head `e721539cff63fc1350c185113fe329ab0eb7848b`, rebased onto `upstream/main` `3e6f7494af16f47e2d9c257f3c16854a340bd147`; source checkout with Node/tsx for the targeted proof command.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
env OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<minimal-temp-config> OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<empty-temp-state-dir> node --import tsx -e 'const { migrateAndPruneGatewaySessionStoreKey, writeGatewaySessionStoreEntry, deleteGatewaySessionStoreEntry, listSessionsFromStore } = await import("./src/gateway/session-utils.ts"); const cfg = { session: { mainKey: "main" }, agents: { list: [{ id: "main", default: true }], defaults: { model: { primary: "openai/gpt-5.4" } } } }; const rawKey = "conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"; const canonicalKey = `agent:main:${rawKey}`; const store = { [rawKey]: { sessionId: "sess-raw", updatedAt: 1, subject: "Alias Needle" } }; const migrated = migrateAndPruneGatewaySessionStoreKey({ cfg, key: rawKey, store }); writeGatewaySessionStoreEntry({ store, primaryKey: migrated.primaryKey, aliasKeys: migrated.preservedAliasKeys, entry: { sessionId: "sess-write", updatedAt: 2, sendPolicy: "deny", subject: "Alias Needle" } }); const listed = listSessionsFromStore({ cfg, storePath: "", store, opts: {} }); const searched = listSessionsFromStore({ cfg, storePath: "", store, opts: { search: "alias needle" } }); const afterWrite = { canonicalSessionId: store[canonicalKey]?.sessionId ?? null, rawSessionId: store[rawKey]?.sessionId ?? null, rawSendPolicy: store[rawKey]?.sendPolicy ?? null, sameEntry: store[canonicalKey] === store[rawKey] }; const visible = { listKeys: listed.sessions.map((session) => session.key), listCount: listed.count, listTotalCount: listed.totalCount, searchKeys: searched.sessions.map((session) => session.key), searchCount: searched.count, searchTotalCount: searched.totalCount }; const deleted = deleteGatewaySessionStoreEntry({ store, primaryKey: migrated.primaryKey, aliasKeys: migrated.preservedAliasKeys }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ primaryKey: migrated.primaryKey, preservedAliasKeys: migrated.preservedAliasKeys, afterWrite, visible, deleted, afterDelete: { canonicalSessionId: store[canonicalKey]?.sessionId ?? null, rawSessionId: store[rawKey]?.sessionId ?? null } }, null, 2));'
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the direct after-fix command:

```json
{
  "primaryKey": "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123",
  "preservedAliasKeys": [
    "conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
  ],
  "afterWrite": {
    "canonicalSessionId": "sess-write",
    "rawSessionId": "sess-write",
    "rawSendPolicy": "deny",
    "sameEntry": true
  },
  "visible": {
    "listKeys": [
      "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
    ],
    "listCount": 1,
    "listTotalCount": 1,
    "searchKeys": [
      "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
    ],
    "searchCount": 1,
    "searchTotalCount": 1
  },
  "deleted": true,
  "afterDelete": {
    "canonicalSessionId": null,
    "rawSessionId": null
  }
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The raw alias and canonical key share the same written entry after replacement, including the patched `sendPolicy`; list and search both return one visible canonical row with `totalCount: 1`; delete removes both keys. The targeted server tests also cover `sessions.patch`, `sessions.delete`, and `sessions.reset` against the real gateway handlers.

**What was not tested:** I did not run a full live chat-channel round trip for this backend store migration. There is no visual UI surface for this fix, so screenshots are not applicable.

Additional checks after the latest rebase:

- `pnpm docs:list` ran before editing.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts` passed: `Test Files 11 passed (11)`, `Tests 546 passed (546)`.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.ts` passed: `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors`.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/session-utils.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.runtime.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts` passed.
- Broad Testbox changed gate was not run because the `blacksmith` CLI is not installed in this environment; `pnpm changed:lanes --json` expanded beyond this PR, so I did not run the broad local gate.

target.title: fix(heartbeat): consume trailing notify=false markers
target.body:
## Summary
- strip only standalone trailing `notify=false` markers from heartbeat fallback replies
- convert that marker into structured silent delivery so Telegram receives `silent: true` instead of visible control text
- keep marker-only replies quiet and preserve inline `notify=false` text as normal user-visible content
- cover relayable exec-completion replies so the legacy completion fallback cannot restore the raw marker

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram heartbeat fallback replies that end with a standalone `notify=false` line should send the user-visible text silently and should not leak `notify=false` into the delivered message.
- Real environment tested: PR branch `b819bd169e` (`fix/telegram-strip-notify-false`), real OpenClaw Telegram channel plugin, gateway restarted after `channels.telegram.enabled=true`, live Telegram Bot API send to Andy's redacted Telegram DM. Bot token and chat id redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec tsx proof-80737-live.ts`
- Evidence after fix: copied live output and redacted runtime log from the post-restart Telegram verification:

```json
{
  "command": "pnpm exec tsx proof-80737-live.ts",
  "rawHeartbeatReply": "PR #80737 live Telegram proof 2026-05-11T18:31:27.376Z\n\nnotify=false",
  "checks": {
    "heartbeatResult": {
      "status": "ran",
      "durationMs": 1514
    },
    "telegramSendCaptured": true,
    "requestText": "PR #80737 live Telegram proof 2026-05-11T18:31:27.376Z",
    "requestHasNotifyFalse": false,
    "requestDisableNotification": true,
    "responseOk": true,
    "responseText": "PR #80737 live Telegram proof 2026-05-11T18:31:27.376Z",
    "responseHasNotifyFalse": false
  },
  "heartbeatEvents": [
    {
      "status": "sent",
      "preview": "PR #80737 live Telegram proof 2026-05-11T18:31:27.376Z",
      "channel": "telegram",
      "silent": true
    }
  ],
  "telegramSendEvidence": {
    "lowLevelSend": {
      "to": "<redacted-telegram-dm>",
      "text": "PR #80737 live Telegram proof 2026-05-11T18:31:27.376Z",
      "options": {
        "silent": true,
        "hasCfg": true
      },
      "result": {
        "messageId": "10",
        "chatId": "<redacted-telegram-dm>"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: Telegram was active, the delivered text did not include `notify=false`, the heartbeat event recorded `silent: true`, the outgoing send used `silent: true` / `disable_notification`, and the live Telegram API returned message id `10`.
- What was not tested: No screenshot of the Telegram app notification surface was captured; this proof uses copied live output, redacted runtime log details, and returned Telegram message metadata.

## Validation
- `pnpm test src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ghost-reminder.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-events-filter.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/infra/outbound/payloads.test.ts src/channels/message/send.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `git diff --check`

Fixes #80569

target.title: fix(config): allow bundled provider timeout overlays
target.body:
Fixes #83201.

## Summary

- Allow bundled model provider config entries to act as overlays, so `models.providers.openai.timeoutSeconds` is valid without redeclaring `baseUrl` and `models`.
- Preserve strict custom-provider validation: unknown provider IDs still require both `baseUrl` and `models`.
- Guard configured-provider fallback against overlay entries without `models`, and prove the timeout overlay is applied during model resolution.

## Real behavior proof

![PR 83267 before/after proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83267-fresh/pr-83267/pr-83267-fix-config-allow-bundled-provider-timeout-overla-before-after-proof.png)

Fresh deterministic proof artifacts:
- Summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83267-fresh/pr-83267/summary.txt
- Before/source proof log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83267-fresh/pr-83267/before-source-proof.log
- After/PR proof log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83267-fresh/pr-83267/after-pr-83267.log

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A documented config such as `models.providers.openai.timeoutSeconds = 600` was rejected at startup because the validator required `models.providers.openai.baseUrl` and `models.providers.openai.models`, even though the entry is only tuning a bundled provider.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Ubuntu 24.04 under WSL, running production config validation and model resolution against real config objects.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a source-level before/after probe against `origin/main` and this PR head, then ran focused validation and changed-file checks:

```text
node --import tsx <provider-timeout-overlay-proof>
CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.google-antigravity.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts
pnpm check:changed
```

**Evidence after fix:** The before/after probe shows the bundled `openai` overlay now validates, while an unknown custom provider with only `timeoutSeconds` still fails:

```text
before_status=10
{
  "overlay": {
    "ok": false,
    "issues": [
      "models.providers.openai.baseUrl: Invalid input: expected string, received undefined",
      "models.providers.openai.models: Invalid input: expected array, received undefined"
    ]
  },
  "custom": {
    "ok": false,
    "issues": [
      "models.providers.custom.baseUrl: Invalid input: expected string, received undefined",
      "models.providers.custom.models: Invalid input: expected array, received undefined"
    ]
  }
}
---
after_status=0
{
  "overlay": {
    "ok": true,
    "issues": []
  },
  "custom": {
    "ok": false,
    "issues": [
      "models.providers.custom.baseUrl: custom model providers must declare baseUrl; provider overlays without baseUrl are only supported for bundled providers",
      "models.providers.custom.models: custom model providers must declare models; provider overlays without models are only supported for bundled providers"
    ]
  }
}
```

Focused tests passed:

```text
src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts: 22 tests passed
src/config/schema.test.ts: 39 tests passed
src/config/config-misc.test.ts: 81 tests passed
src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.test.ts: 84 tests passed in both agents-core and agents-pi-embedded shards
pnpm check:changed: passed
```

**Observed result after fix:** `models.providers.openai.timeoutSeconds` can be used as a standalone bundled provider overlay and the resolver applies it as `requestTimeoutMs=600000`; a custom `models.providers.custom.timeoutSeconds` entry still fails validation until it declares `baseUrl` and `models`.

**What was not tested:** No live OpenAI request was sent; this validates startup config behavior and the model-resolution metadata path without contacting provider APIs.

commands.author: stainlu

target.title: Fix Codex runtime hook and session churn
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes two Codex-runtime stability problems reported by users:

- Fixes #76552: Codex no longer installs the default `PreToolUse` native hook relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, avoiding one `openclaw hooks relay ... --event pre_tool_use` child process per native Codex tool call.
- Fixes #76179: Codex keeps the app-server dynamic tool schema stable across owner/non-owner sender flips. Owner-only tools remain visible to Codex so the dynamic-tool fingerprint does not churn, while unauthorized execution still fails closed.

## Root Cause

For #76552, current `main` already avoided relay subprocesses for `PostToolUse` and finalization hooks when no local handlers exist, but `pre_tool_use` still returned `true` unconditionally in `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts`. Default Codex app-server config could therefore register a `PreToolUse` command hook that shells out through the OpenClaw relay for every native tool use, even when there was no OpenClaw `before_tool_call` policy to run.

For #76179, the Codex app-server thread fingerprint includes the dynamic tool registration set. Owner-only tools were filtered out for non-owner senders, so an owner/non-owner context change changed the registered tool names. That can make Codex start new native app-server sessions and, worse, any per-catalog session workaround can split one OpenClaw conversation across multiple native Codex histories.

## What Changed

- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts` now reports local `pre_tool_use` relay work only when `hasBeforeToolCallPolicy()` is true. `permission_request` remains active because Codex approvals still need the app-server bridge.
- `src/agents/tool-policy.ts` can retain owner-only tools for schema stability while wrapping unauthorized owner-only execution with `Tool restricted to owner senders.`
- `src/agents/pi-tools.ts` exposes that retention option to runtime tool construction.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` passes `retainUnauthorizedOwnerOnlyTools: true` when building Codex dynamic tools, keeping the app-server tool fingerprint stable across sender-owner flips.

## Before / After

Before:

- Default Codex sessions could register a `PreToolUse` relay command even with no `before_tool_call` plugin policy, so multiple native tools could fan out into multiple OpenClaw relay CLI child processes.
- Switching between owner and non-owner senders could change dynamic tool registration and force new Codex native sessions.

After:

- No `PreToolUse` relay command is installed unless there is actual before-tool policy work.
- Command approvals still get the in-process `nativeHookRelay` bridge for `pre_tool_use` decisions.
- Owner-only schemas remain registered for Codex owner and non-owner turns, so the same native Codex thread can resume through interleaved sender-owner changes.
- Unauthorized owner-only tool execution still fails closed.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex runtime process/session churn. Default app-server runs should not install a `PreToolUse` relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, and owner-only dynamic tool availability changes should not restart or split Codex native sessions.
- Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw checkout on May 18, 2026. Private home/workspace paths and auth details are redacted from the older live-run transcript below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: On current PR head `9491522c36e232bb9bda8241288e31bb7c6ac55a`, ran the focused verification commands listed below under `Current-head verification` and `Current-head runtime probe`. Earlier on this PR branch, ran real `pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 ...` turns through the Codex app-server harness.
- Evidence after fix: copied terminal output is embedded below under `Current-head verification`, `Current-head runtime probe`, and `Live Codex app-server smoke`.
- Observed result after fix: the owner/non-owner flip test starts one Codex thread and then resumes that same thread across both non-owner and owner turns; the dynamic tool schema includes both `message` and owner-only `cron`; owner-only execution remains guarded for unauthorized senders; current-head tests and prompt snapshot check pass.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live external chat-channel ingress scenario that naturally flips `senderIsOwner`; that path needs real channel identity setup. The same owner/non-owner availability flip is covered through the production Codex app-server run path and dynamic-tool construction tests.

No product UI screenshot is applicable here because the bugs are runtime process/session churn in the Codex app-server path. Terminal/runtime output is the relevant proof artifact.

### Current-head verification

```text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
9491522c36e232bb9bda8241288e31bb7c6ac55a

$ git diff --stat upstream/main...HEAD
10 files changed, 253 insertions(+), 41 deletions(-)

$ git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
# no output

$ pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.owner-only-gating.test.ts src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  32 passed (32)

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  62 passed (62)

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  203 passed (203)

$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts src/agents/pi-tools.owner-only-gating.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.ts src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts src/agents/tool-policy.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ env OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=extensions pnpm prompt:snapshots:check
Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).
```

### Current-head runtime probe

```json
{
  "head": "9491522c36e232bb9bda8241288e31bb7c6ac55a",
  "hookConfigWithoutBeforeToolPolicy": {
    "featuresHooks": true,
    "preToolUseConfig": [],
    "permissionRequestConfigPresent": false
  },
  "ownerFlipSingleNativeThread": {
    "requestMethods": [
      "thread/start",
      "thread/resume",
      "thread/resume"
    ],
    "startCount": 1,
    "resumeThreadIds": [
      "thread-1",
      "thread-1"
    ],
    "startDynamicToolSample": [
      [
        "message",
        "cron"
      ]
    ],
    "startContainsOwnerOnlyCron": [
      true
    ]
  },
  "unauthorizedOwnerOnlyExecution": {
    "retainedToolNames": [
      "message",
      "cron",
      "gateway",
      "nodes"
    ],
    "unauthorizedCronExecutionError": "Tool restricted to owner senders."
  }
}
```

The runtime probe uses the production Codex app-server run path with a deterministic app-server client harness. It shows the two churn fixes directly: no `PreToolUse` command relay is installed without a before-tool policy, and interleaved owner/non-owner turns use one `thread/start` followed by `thread/resume` calls for the same native thread. It also shows the security boundary: retained owner-only schemas still reject unauthorized execution.

### Live Codex app-server smoke

Earlier on this PR branch, I ran two real local Codex app-server OpenClaw turns with the same session id. That verified the actual app-server harness/auth path and same-thread resume behavior:

```text
$ codex login status
Logged in using ChatGPT

$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 --message "Reply with exactly: OK" --json --timeout 180
[plugins] loading codex from <repo>/dist/extensions/codex/index.js
[agents/auth-profiles] used external cli oauth bootstrap because local oauth was missing or unusable
[agent/embedded] strict-agentic execution contract active: runId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 sessionId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.4 harness=codex configured=unspecified
[agents/harness] agent harness selected
...
"agentHarnessId": "codex"
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "OK"

$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 --message "Reply with exactly: OK2" --json --timeout 180
[plugins] loading codex from <repo>/dist/extensions/codex/index.js
[agents/auth-profiles] used external cli oauth bootstrap because local oauth was missing or unusable
[agent/embedded] strict-agentic execution contract active: runId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 sessionId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.4 harness=codex configured=unspecified
[agents/harness] agent harness selected
...
"agentHarnessId": "codex"
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "OK2"
```

## Regression Coverage

- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts`: no-handler relay state no longer enables `pre_tool_use`; `before_tool_call` still enables `PreToolUse`; `after_tool_call` does not accidentally enable `PreToolUse`.
- `src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts`: retained unauthorized owner-only schemas remain execution-guarded.
- `src/agents/pi-tools.owner-only-gating.test.ts`: Codex-style runtime turns can keep owner-only schemas stable for unauthorized senders.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`: Codex dynamic tool construction opts into retained owner-only schemas, and owner/non-owner flips resume one native Codex thread instead of splitting history.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts`: side-question/permission-request coverage keeps permission relay behavior while confirming default `PreToolUse` stays empty when no before-tool policy exists.

Broad Testbox `pnpm check:changed` was not run because the Blacksmith/Testbox CLI/env/profile were not available in this environment. The focused affected suites above are green locally, and the latest push triggers exact-head GitHub CI.

target.title: fix(models): label picker auth via effective provider order
target.body:
## Summary
- Pass the effective OpenAI/Codex accepted auth provider set into the shared /models provider label path.
- Keep the change display-only: model selection and dispatch routing are unchanged.
- Add regression coverage for an OpenAI picker page that should display the Codex OAuth profile instead of falling back to OPENAI_API_KEY.

Fixes #83574.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: The `/models` provider header for `openai` should label the effective Codex OAuth auth profile selected by `auth.order.openai`, instead of implying an API-key fallback such as `api-key (env: OPENAI_API_KEY)`.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, Node v25.9.0, with the real `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.ts` implementation and a real temporary `auth-profiles.json` store under `$TMP_HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/agent`. The store contained only `openai-codex:user@example.com` as an OAuth profile; there was no `openai:default` API-key profile.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the real header formatter through `node --import tsx` with `provider: "openai"`, `auth.order.openai: ["openai-codex:user@example.com"]`, and `models.providers.openai.baseUrl: "https://api.openai.com/v1"`.

```bash
TMP_HOME=$(mktemp -d)
AGENT_DIR="$TMP_HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/agent"
mkdir -p "$AGENT_DIR"
HOME="$TMP_HOME" AGENT_DIR="$AGENT_DIR" node --import tsx --eval '
  import { formatModelsAvailableHeader } from "./src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.ts";
  const cfg = {
    auth: { order: { openai: ["openai-codex:user@example.com"] } },
    models: { providers: { openai: { baseUrl: "https://api.openai.com/v1" } } }
  };
  console.log(formatModelsAvailableHeader({
    provider: "openai",
    total: 2,
    cfg,
    agentId: "main",
    agentDir: process.env.AGENT_DIR,
    workspaceDir: process.cwd(),
  }));
'
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the real OpenClaw formatter path:

```text
Models (openai · 🔑 oauth (openai-codex:user@example.com)) — 2 available
```

Observed result after fix: The provider picker header now shows `oauth (openai-codex:user@example.com)` for `openai`, proving the display follows the effective Codex OAuth auth path and does not fall back to `api-key (env: OPENAI_API_KEY)` for this configuration.

Not tested: Live Telegram button rendering was not exercised; the Telegram callback uses this same shared `formatModelsAvailableHeader` path, and the change is covered at that shared layer.

## Verification
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth-label.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.test.ts src/agents/model-auth-label.test.ts
- pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.test.ts src/agents/model-auth-label.test.ts
